label: US Troops Deployed
source: US Adjutant General records / K. Jack Bauer, The Mexican War

label: US Killed in Action
source: US War Department records / US National Archives

label: US Deaths from Disease
source: US Surgeon General records / K. Jack Bauer
contestNote: Estimates range from 10,000–13,000; dysentery and yellow fever were primary killers

label: Territory Ceded by Mexico
source: Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo — US National Archives
contestNote: Represents ~55% of Mexico's pre-war territory; some estimates range 491,000–529,000 sq mi
delta: Including California, Nevada, Utah, most of AZ, NM, CO

label: US Indemnity to Mexico
source: Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo, Article XII
delta: Plus $3.25M in assumed claims against Mexico

label: Mexican Military Deaths
source: Archivo de la Secretaría de Defensa Nacional / Various historians
contestNote: Estimates range from 25,000–50,000; official records were poorly kept during the conflict

label: Alamo Defenders Killed
source: Texas State Library and Archives Commission
contestNote: Counts range from 182–189; exact number debated by historians due to incomplete rosters

label: Goliad Massacre Victims
source: Texas State Library and Archives — Fannin's Command Records
contestNote: Some sources cite 330–342; a small number escaped

label: Major Engagements
source: US Army Center of Military History
delta: Across Texas Revolution and Mexican-American War theaters

label: Mexico's Territory Lost
source: Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo / Griswold del Castillo
contestNote: Including earlier Texas loss; some calculations cite 47% using different baseline figures

label: Battle of San Jacinto Duration
source: Sam Houston dispatch — Texas State Archives
delta: Decisive victory over 3× larger Mexican force
